question: Is the large colorful abstract painting now positioned on the right side of the gallery wall?
Are the two smaller gold-framed paintings now positioned on the left side of the gallery wall?
Is the portrait painting (man in dark clothing) now positioned above the landscape painting on the left wall?
Are the two smaller paintings vertically aligned in a stack (one directly above the other) rather than side-by-side or offset?
Has the large abstract painting been completely removed from its original position on the left (no duplicates)?
Is the artistic content of the large abstract painting (brushstrokes, colors) preserved without hallucinated changes or distortion?
Is the content of the two smaller paintings (the portrait figure and the landscape scenery) preserved accurately in their new locations?
Are the aspect ratios and relative sizes of all three paintings preserved (not noticeably stretched or squashed)?
Are the museum visitors and the sculpture on the pedestal (right side) preserved without distortion or accidental deletion?
Does the large abstract painting on the right follow the correct wall perspective (vanishing point) rather than looking flat or pasted on?
Is the placement of the large painting on the right physically plausible (not cutting through the sculpture, bench, or visitors)?
Is the wall surface free from 'ghosting' artifacts, leftover textures, or dark rectangles where the paintings were originally located?
Are the edges of the relocated paintings clean and integrated, without jagged cutouts or halos?
